behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: The failure is at a module handoff boundary: source collectors -> aggregation/orchestration -> summary input payload. A unit test of one function can miss dropped/overwritten sections when modules are wired together. Real OS/network is not required to reproduce this specific omission bug; in-process fakes for the three sources are sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real aggregation/orchestration modules with three deterministic fake source adapters and a spy/stub summary generator. Execute one report run and assert the summary-generator input contains metrics, issue tracker, and deployment-log sections (with expected keys from each adapter).

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module error-propagation and fallback behavior (source adapter failure -> orchestrator -> report section fallback). A pure unit test is too narrow, but real infrastructure is not required because the outage can be represented by a failing adapter stub/exception. No AI-output-quality dependency is required for this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run the report pipeline with two healthy fake sources and one source adapter that raises a timeout/unavailable error. Assert the run completes, output includes a `data unavailable` marker for that section, and other sections still render.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on the model’s actual summarization judgment (faithfulness/accuracy), which a deterministic mock would hide. Integration can verify routing/payload shape but cannot catch real generation mistakes (omission, distortion, hallucinated values). Rejected higher level Workflow because behavior text specifies a single summary-generation decision, not multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Provide representative input datasets with known key values, call the real summary-generation model, and evaluate whether the produced narrative preserves those values (e.g., expected numeric facts/entities present and no contradictory values). Run across repeated trials/fixtures to detect non-deterministic degradation.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Workflow rejected: behavior covers one agentic summarization step, not a multi-step agent pipeline.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary includes real external I/O (HTTP/internal APIs + email transport/inbox delivery). In-process mocks can hide transport/auth/format/inbox-integration failures. Rejected Agentic as minimum because this behavior’s acceptance criterion is end-to-end delivery to inbox, not model output quality; summary content can be fixed/stubbed for this test.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the script end-to-end in a production-like environment against reachable test instances (or sandbox equivalents) of the three data sources and email service. Assert a message is delivered to the configured recipient mailbox with the expected report artifact/structure.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Agentic rejected: behavior asserts delivery success, not correctness of model decisions.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: This spans persistence across executions plus retry under real service unreachability conditions. The critical failure often appears at OS/process/network boundaries (file durability/path/permissions, process restart state reload, real connection failure semantics). A mocked or single-process test can miss those failures.
behaviors.test_description: Run 1: execute script with email endpoint intentionally unreachable; assert report is persisted to disk in retry storage. Run 2 (separate execution, next schedule simulation) with email restored; assert persisted report is loaded and successfully delivered, and retry artifact state is updated/cleared.
